concerns.text: T018 ('Implement high drift flagging logic...') is not executable because it requires a block permutation p-value, but the task to generate the null distribution (T020) and the significance test logic (T023) are in later phases. The task description does not specify how to obtain the p-value without executing the downstream tasks, creating a circular dependency for the implementer.

T014 ('Implement window iteration loop...') lacks the explicit instruction to integrate the R² < 0.8 skip logic defined in FR-003b. Without this specific instruction, an implementer might process all windows regardless of model failure, violating the spec's edge case handling and producing invalid drift metrics.

T015 ('Add error handling for feature variance...') does not explicitly state that the variance check must occur AFTER the median imputation defined in T010/FR-001. An implementer might check variance on raw data, leading to incorrect feature dropping or skipping the imputation step entirely.

T020 ('Implement Null Model Baseline generation...') is too coarse. It combines generating the shuffled baseline, recalculating importance rankings, and comparing them. This should be split into: (1) Shuffling window order, (2) Re-running importance calculation on shuffled data, (3) Comparing metrics. The current granularity risks non-deterministic implementation.

T026 ('Implement final report generation...') is too coarse. It combines aggregating metrics and writing JSON. It should be split into: (1) Aggregation logic to compute global stats from CSVs, (2) JSON serialization and file write. This ensures the aggregation logic is testable independently of the I/O.
